repair_history.diagnosis:
line 14: left and right sides of binary expression '>=' are of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' and 'real', but must be of the same type: Comparison of opaque port type with real literal -> Replace Target_Speed >= 0.0 with Target_Speed.val >= 0.0
line 15: left and right sides of binary expression '<' are of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' and 'real', but must be of the same type: Comparison of opaque port type with real literal -> Replace Target_Speed < 150.0 with Target_Speed.val < 150.0
line 17: left and right sides of binary expression '=' are of type 'Types::state_sig.impl' and 'int', but must be of the same type: Comparison of opaque port type with integer literal -> Replace State_Signal = 1 with State_Signal.val = 1
line 18: left side of binary expression '<=' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Comparison of opaque port type with real literal -> Replace Actual_Speed <= Target_Speed with Actual_Speed.val <= Target_Speed.val
line 19: left side of binary expression '-' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type 'int' or 'real': Subtraction of opaque port types -> Replace Actual_Speed - Actual_Speed' with Actual_Speed.val - Actual_Speed'.val

line 19: Couldn't resolve reference to 'val'.: AGREE cannot resolve '.val' on opaque data types -> Replace 'Actual_Speed.val' with 'Actual_Speed' and 'Actual_Speed'.val with 'Actual_Speed' in the acceleration_constraint guarantee
line 19: The condition of the if statement is of type 'RealTypeDef' but must be of type 'bool': Invalid boolean expression in if-then-else -> Rewrite the if expression to use proper boolean comparison with '>= 0' as a boolean expression
line 19: left and right sides of binary expression '-' are of type 'real' and '<error>', but must be of the same type: Invalid expression with unresolved type -> Replace the complex expression with a simpler valid AGREE expression using only visible identifiers
line 33: 'Actual' and 'Actual_Speed' have incompatible classifiers.: Port reference mismatch in connection -> Change 'THROT.Actual' to 'THROT.Actual_Speed' in the connection
line 35: The types of 'Target_Speed' and 'Target' do not match.: Port reference mismatch in connection -> Change 'CNTRL.Target' to 'CNTRL.Target_Speed' in the connection

line 19: left and right sides of binary expression '<=' are of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' and 'real', but must be of the same type: AGREE comparison between port type and real literal -> Change 'Actual_Speed <= max_accel' to 'Actual_Speed.val <= max_accel'
line 19: left side of binary expression '<=' is of type 'Types::speed.speed_impl' but must be of type'int' or 'real': AGREE comparison between port type and real literal -> Change 'Actual_Speed <= max_accel' to 'Actual_Speed.val <= max_accel'

/CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 33: 'Actual' and 'Actual_Speed' have incompatible classifiers.: Type mismatch between THROT.Actual and Actual_Speed port -> Remove the connection AcSpeedToTop as it references implementation port 'THROT.Actual' in a component type contract
/CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 35: The types of 'Target_Speed' and 'Target' do not match.: Type mismatch between Target_Speed port and CNTRL.Target port -> Remove the connection TgSpeedToCntrl as it references implementation port 'CNTRL.Target' in a component type contract
/CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 40: 'State_Out' and 'State_Signal' have incompatible classifiers.: Type mismatch between SM.State_Out and State_Signal port -> Remove the connection SSToSM as it references implementation port 'SM.State_Out' in a component type contract

line 19: Couldn't resolve reference to 'abs'.: abs function not recognized in AGREE context -> Replace abs(Actual_Speed.val - Actual_Speed.val_previous) with (Actual_Speed.val - Actual_Speed.val_previous)
line 19: Couldn't resolve reference to 'val_previous'.: val_previous not a valid AGREE expression -> Replace Actual_Speed.val_previous with a valid AGREE temporal reference or remove the dependency
line 19: left and right sides of binary expression '-' are of type 'real' and '<error>', but must be of the same type: Type mismatch in subtraction expression -> Ensure both operands of '-' are of type 'real'
line 19: left and right sides of binary expression '<' are of type '<error>' and 'real', but must be of the same type: Type mismatch in comparison expression -> Ensure both operands of '<' are of type 'real'
